title: Codex CLI: Refactoring Legacy Code and Writing Tests
description: Use Codex CLI to systematically refactor legacy code, improve readability, and auto-generate unit tests for existing functions.

resources.title: Codex CLI Advanced Usage
resources.description: Multi-turn sessions, file context, and automation patterns for Codex CLI

resources.title: OpenAI Prompt Engineering Guide
resources.description: Techniques for writing effective prompts for code transformation tasks

resources.title: Refactor a legacy function with Codex CLI
resources.description: Use Codex CLI to refactor a complex function and generate its unit tests in one session
resources.command: codex 'Refactor this function to use async/await, add JSDoc comments, and generate Jest unit tests for all edge cases' --file legacy.js

resources.title: Codex CLI Refactoring and Testing Workflow
resources.search_keyword: Codex CLI refactoring legacy code unit tests tutorial 2026

quiz.question: What is the main goal of refactoring in this lesson?
options: Improve readability and structure without changing behavior
Replace all functions with AI-generated code
quiz.explanation: Refactoring improves clarity and maintainability while keeping the code's behavior the same.

quiz.question: Why should you generate tests during a refactoring workflow?
options: To prove the old code was useless
To check that behavior stays stable after changes
To remove the need for debugging forever
quiz.explanation: Tests protect against regressions and make refactoring safer.

quiz.question: Which type of code is usually the best first target for tests?
options: Critical business logic and utility functions
quiz.explanation: Critical logic gives the highest value because failures there affect real behavior.
